commit.message:
fix: gate all dashboard page fetches on useAuth().orgId

Every client page was fetching on mount before the Clerk JWT included
orgId, returning empty data. Adding orgId from useAuth() as a dependency
ensures the API is only called once Clerk has confirmed the active org.

Pages fixed: jobs, candidates, pipeline, inbox, analytics, roles/[id]

and PipelineStage enum. Implement EventBus interface with in-process
EventEmitter backend for pipeline orchestration and SSE fan-out.

all imports. Add snake_case file naming rule to coding-principles.md.

commit.message:
🐛 fix: inline workflow content for global installs

@-file references in Claude Code command files resolve relative to the
project root. For global installs (~/.claude), workflow files live outside
any project so all commands failed to load them. The installer now inlines
workflow content directly into command files at global install time.

Also bumps to v0.1.1.

commit.message:
feat: multi-channel notification system with QR integrations

Implements full multi-channel notification infrastructure across DB,
backend, and frontend — closing the gap from email-only (60%) to a
complete system supporting in-app, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack, Teams,
and Google Meet.

## DB (migration 0015)
- Extends notification_channel enum: SMS, IN_APP, WHATSAPP, SLACK, TEAMS
- Creates notification_preferences (quiet hours, digest mode, per-channel toggles)
- Creates in_app_notifications (with unread index)
- Creates device_tokens (FCM push, per platform)
- Creates connected_integrations (nonce QR, OAuth tokens AES-256-GCM encrypted)
- Adds phone_number column to unified_profiles

## Backend (notification_service)
- HTTP server on port 3012 (Axum) + Kafka consumer run concurrently
- 18 API routes: notifications CRUD, preferences upsert, device tokens,
  WhatsApp/Slack/Teams/Google OAuth and webhook flows
- Fanout dispatchers: in_app, SMS (Twilio), push (FCM), WhatsApp, Slack,
  Teams Adaptive Cards — all gated on notification_preferences + quiet hours
- 5 new Kafka topic subscriptions: EscrowRelease, PayoutVeto, MatchResult,
  WarrantyClaimed, MentorshipPaired
- AES-256-GCM encryption for OAuth access/refresh tokens at rest

## Frontend
- notifications/page.tsx: loads from API on mount, 30s unread count polling,
  optimistic mark-read, graceful fallback to demo data
- notification-settings/page.tsx: loads/saves preferences via API, new
  Connected Integrations panel with QR codes (WhatsApp, Slack, Google Meet)
  and webhook URL paste (Teams); polls integration status every 5s
- api.ts: InAppNotification, NotifPrefs, IntegrationStatus types +
  fetchInAppNotifications, fetchUnreadCount, markNotificationRead,
  markAllNotificationsRead, fetchNotificationPreferences,
  saveNotificationPreferences, registerDeviceToken, fetchIntegrationsStatus,
  initWhatsAppConnect, saveTeamsWebhook, disconnectIntegration
- next.config.ts: proxy rewrites for /api/notifications, /api/notification-preferences,
  /api/device-tokens, /api/integrations → localhost:3012
- docker-compose.yml: port 3012 exposed + 12 new env vars (Twilio, FCM,
  Slack, Google, encryption key) with dev_placeholder defaults
- docs/secret-registry.md: 10 new secrets registered
- .gitignore: add .env.local and .env.*.local patterns
- .claude/launch.json: autoPort: true for dev server

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fix origin.city → origin.label in ShareItinerary route prop
- Fix BigInt literal syntax for ES2020 compat in XMTP chat panel

Flesh out config centralization and CI TODO items

Config centralization now describes two distinct layers: infra globals
(PROJECT/ZONE/etc) and game-specific config (WORLD_NAME, ports, paths),
with a note that per-game sourced config files are the right destination
for layer 2.

CI expanded from validate-only to two tiers: syntax/validate (free) plus
design/contract tests — shellcheck, systemd pairing checks, Requires+After
verification, template placeholder audits, shutdown sequence checks.

fix(overnight): unset CLAUDECODE before claude invocation

Claude refuses to launch inside another Claude session (nested session guard).
Running the script from a Claude terminal inherited CLAUDECODE env var, causing
every claude -p call to exit immediately with 0 bytes and no error message.

Fix: unset CLAUDECODE in the subshell before timeout/claude invocation.

commit.message:
fix: use useAuth().orgId instead of useOrganization() for dashboard fetch gate

useAuth().orgId directly reflects the current JWT — when non-null the server
will also see orgId via auth(). useOrganization().organization can be truthy
before the JWT has been refreshed, causing the API to still return zeros.

fix(overnight): continue-on-failure + auto-kill stalled children

the text path instead of binary.

commit.message:
feat: reaction-aware rating chips (positive, mixed, negative)

Rating sheet now adapts tag sections based on the gut reaction:
- Obsessed/Enjoyed: "What stood out?" (positive chips)
- Mixed: both "What stood out?" and "What fell short?"
- Not me: "What fell short?" (negative chips only)

Added SHORTCOMING_TAGS per place type (hotel, restaurant, etc.)
with critical feedback options like Overpriced, Poor service, etc.
New shortcomingTags field on PlaceRating flows through to the DB
(JSONB, no migration needed) and interaction tracking metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix project creation: remove nonexistent created_by column

The insert sent created_by which doesn't exist in the projects
table (the actual column is owner_id with DEFAULT auth.uid()).
PostgREST rejected it with 400. Removed the field entirely since
the database sets owner_id automatically.

Also added error display to the create project form so insert
failures are visible to the user instead of silently swallowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace unreliable Odoo webhook with poll-based KDS notification

Odoo's base.automation webhook (on_write trigger) stopped firing
reliably — KDS OWL module bypasses ORM write() intermittently.

New approach: KDS poll script (already running every 3s) now tracks
pos.prep.state stage changes and fires webhooks via a relay endpoint.

- Enhanced KDS_POLL_SCRIPT: tracks state→stage mapping, fires webhook
  on PREPARING/READY stage transitions
- Added /_he_kds_notify relay in KDS proxy: adds KDS_WEBHOOK_SECRET
  server-side, forwards to existing whatsapp webhook handler
- Double-notification protection: client _firedSet + server notified_counters
- Preparation display detection: no page reload on interactive screens
- Fixed getDisplayId() to also check preparation_display URL param

Staff must access prep display through proxy:
hamzaexpress.in/kds/pos_preparation_display/web?display_id=17

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add per-API-key breakdown to Claude API tab

New backend: fetch usage grouped by api_key_id+model, resolve key names
via /v1/organizations/api_keys, calculate costs via pricing.js (cost
endpoint doesn't support group_by api_key_id). All 4 API requests run
in parallel. Cache key upgraded to anthropic-dashboard-v2.

New frontend: horizontal stacked bar chart (cost per key by model),
key comparison table (tokens, input, output, cache %, cost, last used),
key usage timeline (stacked area, only shown when >1 key active).
Full i18n (DE/EN) and period filtering support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Token usage dashboard for Claude Code — tracks costs, sessions, models, and tools with real-time updates

commit.message:
fix(nginx): HTTPS on pour PHP-FPM derrière reverse proxy SSL

Le TLS se termine au Nginx hôte. Sans fastcgi_param HTTPS on, PHP croit
être en HTTP et request()->url() retourne http://, causant un mismatch
de signature sur les URLs signées Livewire (upload-file → 401).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: minimap click-to-line navigation and word-based text wrapping

1. Minimap click now navigates to the line at the click position (using
   startLine + y/LINE_HEIGHT_PX) instead of computing a scroll offset
   from slider ratios. For large files where the minimap scrolls, the
   old approach caused huge jumps away from the clicked position. Drag
   offset is eliminated by recomputing the layout after the jump.

2. Changed text wrapping from character-based (word-break: break-all)
   to word-based (overflow-wrap: break-word). Added countWordWrapLines()
   to line-projection that simulates CSS word-wrap behavior: breaks at
   spaces normally, falls back to character-break for words longer than
   a line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: PydanticUserError, Dynaconf MLflow tests, SQLite lock (#561)

* fix(overnight): strict full-test-suite + no-skip protocol across all batch scripts

Every overnight script now enforces identical COMPLETION PROTOCOL:

STEP 1: uv run pytest tests/ -x -q (FULL suite, loop until exit 0)
  - Diagnose + fix every failure, re-run from scratch each time
  - BANNED: -k filter, --ignore, xfail/skip markers to hide failures

STEP 2: uv run pre-commit run --all-files (loop until exit 0)
  - Fix every hook failure, re-run --all-files each time
  - BANNED: --no-verify, SKIP= env var, bypassing any hook

STEP 3: push + gh pr create — ONLY after both steps above are fully green

Also applies stream-json + timeout to overnight-master-plan,
overnight-prefect-docker-monai, overnight-script-consolidation,
and batch-script-executor (previously still using stdbuf / buffered output).

All 6 files pass bash -n syntax check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: PydanticUserError, Dynaconf MLflow tests, SQLite lock (Closes #540, #541, #558)

T1 (#540): Move DeployConfig import to module level in deploy_flow.py so
Prefect's Pydantic flow model can resolve it (was in TYPE_CHECKING only).

T2 (#541): Replace settings.MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI references in Dynaconf tests
with resolve_tracking_uri() calls — per Rule #22, MLflow URI comes from env
var only, never from Dynaconf TOML.

T3 (#558): Refactor TestDataFlowMlflowFunctional to use tmp_path + monkeypatch.setenv()
instead of tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() + direct os.environ manipulation,
preventing Prefect/MLflow telemetry SQLite lock collisions. Also update
test_data_flow_opens_mlflow_experiment to check for EXPERIMENT_DATA constant
instead of the bare string literal (data_flow.py uses resolve_experiment_name).

101/101 tests passing. Ruff + mypy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct contractor trade classification for CF Evans and Feyen/Zylstra

CF Evans (GC) was appearing under Fire/Sprinkler because permit-category
counting only tallied mapped trade categories, so a single fire-sprinkler
permit outweighed many untracked GC permits.

Feyen/Zylstra (Electrical) was falling through to General Contractor
default because their name contains no electrical keyword.

Both fixed via TRADE_OVERRIDES which takes priority over all other logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
